2026-04-17T03:30:23.8979937Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8980770Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8981444Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8983044Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8983547Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8983922Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8984183Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8984476Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8984749Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8985110Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8985432Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8985876Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T03:30:23.8986298Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.Network`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 2404cbdff (PR #58200) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe 1.1.0 stable release of `Azure.Provisioning.Network` added significant new functionality:\n- Expanded from 37 to 120 Network resource types\n- Added polymorphic type support for `BaseAdminRule` (`NetworkAdminRule`, `NetworkDefaultAdminRule`), `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`, and `FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes\n- Added support for 6 new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Empty `Authenticate the Client` section** — The heading exists in the README but has no content at all\n- **Empty `Next steps` section** — The heading exists but contains no links or guidance\n- **No examples for `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources** — The 6 new NSP resource types (`NetworkSecurityPerimeter`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAssociation`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLink`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLoggingConfiguration`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile`) added in 1.1.0 are undocumented in the README\n- **No examples for polymorphic types** — The new `BaseAdminRule`/`NetworkAdminRule`/`NetworkDefaultAdminRule` hierarchy and `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`/`FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes have no README coverage despite being prominent 1.1.0 features\n- **Thin `Key concepts` section** — With the library now covering 120 resource types, the key concepts section should enumerate major resource categories and note the polymorphic type pattern\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` — **Edit**\n\n1. Fill in the `Authenticate the Client` section (currently empty after the header). Add a note that this is a provisioning/infrastructure library using `DefaultAzureCredential` via `azd` and does not require client-side authentication at resource definition time, e.g.:\n\n```markdown\n### Authenticate the client\n\nThis library is used for defining infrastructure declaratively in .NET and generating Bicep templates for deployment via [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview). Authentication happens at deployment time through `azd`; no client credential is required when defining resources in code.\n```\n\n2. Expand the `Key concepts` section to mention the resource count and polymorphic type pattern:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\n\n`Azure.Provisioning.Network` provides over 120 Network resource types including virtual networks, subnets, load balancers, firewalls, NAT gateways, network security groups, network watchers, and more.\n\n**Polymorphic types:** Some resources support polymorphic hierarchies. For example, `BaseAdminRule` is the base type with `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as concrete subtypes. Similarly, `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo` and `FirewallPolicyRule` follow this pattern.\n```\n\n3. Add a new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` example in the `Examples` section.\n\n4. Add a `Next steps` content entry:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\n- Learn more about [Azure Provisioning libraries for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\n- Use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/) to deploy your infrastructure\n- Browse additional [Azure.Provisioning.*](https://www.nuget.org/packages?q=Azure.Provisioning) packages for other Azure services\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nWrite sample code in a new or existing test file. The existing file to add regions to is:\n`sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/tests/BasicNetworkTests.cs`\n\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate` region that demonstrates creating a `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` with at least one child resource (e.g., `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile` or `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`). Wrap any test-only setup/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\n\nAdd a new test method with a `#region Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic` region demonstrating defining a `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as subtypes of `BaseAdminRule`.\n\nThen add the corresponding README snippet placeholders in the Examples section:\n\n```markdown\n### Create a Network Security Perimeter\n\n```C# Snippet:NetworkSecurityPerimeterCreate\n```\n```\n\n```markdown\n### Use polymorphic admin rules\n\n```C# Snippet:BaseAdminRulePolymorphic\n```\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` currently has all required sections but two are empty:\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---|---|\n| Getting started \u003e Install the package | ✅ |\n| Getting started \u003e Prerequisites | ✅ |\n| Getting started \u003e Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Too brief for v1.1.0 scope |\n| Examples | ⚠️ Missing 1.1.0 feature examples |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ |\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty |\n| Contributing | ✅ |\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 network` — reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects the code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 network` — regenerates public API listing files. Required if any public types or members were added or changed\n5. `dotnet format sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/` — ensures consistent code formatting\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[Network] Docs: Azure.Provisioning.Network README missing 1.1.0 feature examples and incomplete sections"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-17T03:34:03.3771712Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3771846Z **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.Network`
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3772334Z **Service directory:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/`
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3772725Z **Triggered by:** commit 2404cbdff (PR #58200) by @ArcturusZhang
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3772951Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3773032Z ## What Changed
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3773148Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3773405Z The 1.1.0 stable release of `Azure.Provisioning.Network` added significant new functionality:
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3773857Z - Expanded from 37 to 120 Network resource types
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3774590Z - Added polymorphic type support for `BaseAdminRule` (`NetworkAdminRule`, `NetworkDefaultAdminRule`), `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`, and `FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3775374Z - Added support for 6 new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3775657Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3775737Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3775846Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3776225Z - **Empty `Authenticate the Client` section** — The heading exists in the README but has no content at all
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3776885Z - **Empty `Next steps` section** — The heading exists but contains no links or guidance
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3778588Z - **No examples for `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` resources** — The 6 new NSP resource types (`NetworkSecurityPerimeter`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAccessRule`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterAssociation`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLink`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterLoggingConfiguration`, `NetworkSecurityPerimeterProfile`) added in 1.1.0 are undocumented in the README
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3780765Z - **No examples for polymorphic types** — The new `BaseAdminRule`/`NetworkAdminRule`/`NetworkDefaultAdminRule` hierarchy and `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo`/`FirewallPolicyRule` subtypes have no README coverage despite being prominent 1.1.0 features
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3782385Z - **Thin `Key concepts` section** — With the library now covering 120 resource types, the key concepts section should enumerate major resource categories and note the polymorphic type pattern
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3783147Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3783228Z <details>
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3783550Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3783782Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3784099Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3784482Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3784572Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3784708Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3784952Z **File:** `sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md` — **Edit**
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3785213Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3786016Z 1. Fill in the `Authenticate the Client` section (currently empty after the header). Add a note that this is a provisioning/infrastructure library using `DefaultAzureCredential` via `azd` and does not require client-side authentication at resource definition time, e.g.:
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3786868Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3786948Z ```markdown
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3787137Z ### Authenticate the client
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3787429Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3788515Z This library is used for defining infrastructure declaratively in .NET and generating Bicep templates for deployment via [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview). Authentication happens at deployment time through `azd`; no client credential is required when defining resources in code.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3789695Z ```
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3789794Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3790049Z 2. Expand the `Key concepts` section to mention the resource count and polymorphic type pattern:
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3790389Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3790462Z ```markdown
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3790639Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3790753Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3791382Z This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3792079Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3792802Z `Azure.Provisioning.Network` provides over 120 Network resource types including virtual networks, subnets, load balancers, firewalls, NAT gateways, network security groups, network watchers, and more.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3793471Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3794355Z **Polymorphic types:** Some resources support polymorphic hierarchies. For example, `BaseAdminRule` is the base type with `NetworkAdminRule` and `NetworkDefaultAdminRule` as concrete subtypes. Similarly, `FirewallPolicyRuleCollectionInfo` and `FirewallPolicyRule` follow this pattern.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3795368Z ```
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3795465Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3795656Z 3. Add a new `NetworkSecurityPerimeter` example in the `Examples` section.
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3795935Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3796027Z 4. Add a `Next steps` content entry:

2026-04-17T03:34:03.3796549Z 
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3797030Z - Learn more about [Azure Provisioning libraries for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3797936Z - Use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/) to deploy your infrastructure
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3798750Z - Browse additional [Azure.Provisioning.*](https://www.nuget.org/packages?q=Azure.Provisioning) packages for other Azure services
2026-04-17T03:34:03.3799251Z ```
